There was a time when every girl I met felt electric. Every glance, every laugh and suddenly, I was off to the races. I’d catch myself daydreaming as I walked across campus, just picturing what it’d feel like to hold her hand, to kiss her, to call her mine. It wasn’t really about love, if I’m honest. The fantasy. The feeling that I was wanted.

But something’s changed.

Now, I leave the library at nine, tired but satisfied. I see couples holding hands, whispering to each other under the streetlights. And instead of feeling jealous, or even left out, I just feel... almost sorry. Not in a mean way. More like, I see myself in them—chasing after something I just don’t want anymore.

For a while, I wondered if something was wrong with me. Why don’t I care about the chase? Why am I not driven by that old hunger?

Turns out, nothing’s wrong. I’m just growing up.

Here’s what’s different:
- I want real connection now;something deeper, not just quick hits of excitement.
- My faith is shaping where I’m headed. Lust doesn’t fit with that. Purpose does.
- I’m focused—on tech, on making a difference, on becoming the man I’m meant to be.
- I see through the noise. Not every couple is in love. Not every kiss means peace.

This shift isn’t a loss—it’s clarity. I’m not uninterested in women, just done with the act. I don’t want to chase shadows while I’m busy building something real. I want a connection that fits with my path, a relationship that adds to my growth instead of pulling me away from it. And until that happens, I’m fine walking solo. I’m not really alone anyway—I’m walking with purpose.

To the guys still chasing, 
I get it. I’ve been there. Maybe you think I’m just burned out or over-thinking things. But maybe you feel it, that quiet nudge toward something more. Toward being more than just someone’s lover. Toward being a leader.

Here’s where I’m at: building, coding, mentoring, worshiping. Dreaming about flying and seeing the world from above. I’m not chasing girls anymore.I’m chasing what God has for me. And when love does show up, it won’t be another chase. It’ll feel like coming home.
